title: Amauropelta minima
taxonomy: Amauropelta minima is classified as a species of fern within the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Amauropelta and is native to South America. The classification of this plant is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic similarity to other species within the family. intro: Amauropelta minima, also known as the Chilean maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
